candidate.title: Dayu Paratus DPU software-stack and cloud-integration boundary evidence

local_checks.raw_manifest_scan: Read the two existing local Dayu product-page captures for Paratus 1.0 and Paratus 2.0. The June 27 and July 6 Paratus 1.0 captures share the same content hash as the selected June 28 Paratus 1.0 source, and the June 27 Paratus 2.0 capture shares the same content hash as the selected June 28 Paratus 2.0 source. No new external fetch was used.
local_checks.wiki_search: Searched Network Storage Cluster Infrastructure, Compute Accelerator Parameter Comparison, AI Infra Coverage Map, coverage-map.json, loop-state.json, ingest.md, and prior parent/r10 gap proofs for Dayu, Paratus, DPDK, SPDK, OpenStack, Kubernetes, HPRT, FPGA, RDMA, Corigine, Yusur, Resnics, Asterfusion, JaguarMicro, and BlueField. Before parent-22, Dayu appeared as crawl-inventory and no-parameter comparison evidence only, while prior DPU/SmartNIC slices had already covered the other vendors.
local_checks.domain_index_scan: ai_infra wiki/index.md routes this work to Network Storage Cluster Infrastructure, Compute Accelerator Parameter Comparison, and AI Infra Coverage Map. No standalone Dayu product page is needed because the source supports only a bounded qualitative boundary update.
local_checks.structured_catalog_scan: data/compute_accelerators schema, SKU, observation, and resolved files have no Dayu structured row. The selected Dayu captures contain qualitative software, offload, and cloud-integration wording but no schema-supported numeric fields with explicit product boundaries.
local_checks.prior_manifest_scan: Parent-1 covers Corigine Agilio CX; parent-3 covers Yusur K2-Pro and SWIFT-2200N Pro; parent-7 covers Resnics Stargate-N2025; parent-9 covers Asterfusion Helium and CX102S-DPU; parent-15 covers JaguarMicro Yunxiao; parent-18 covers NVIDIA BlueField-4; r10 task-3 covers Resnics Stargate-N1025 and Stargate-S1100. Parent-21 covers Huawei Atlas 800T A3 aggregate fields. None of these promote Dayu Paratus software/offload or OpenStack/Kubernetes integration facts.

duplicate_boundary_checks.result: Corigine Agilio CX is already represented as a SmartNIC product-brief catalog slice; parent-22 does not repeat its port, memory, or PCIe fields.
Yusur K2-Pro and SWIFT-2200N Pro are already represented as DPU catalog/comparison boundaries; parent-22 does not repeat DOE, NoC, NVMe-oF, latency, jitter, or network-bandwidth evidence.

Resnics Stargate-N2025, N1025, and S1100 product-specific fields are prior DPU/SmartNIC/storage-offload slices; parent-22 does not repeat their memory, host-interface, power, IOPS, latency, or protocol wording.
Asterfusion Helium and CX102S-DPU are prior DPU catalog boundaries; parent-22 does not repeat Helium mixed-service capability, memory, host-interface, power, or CX102S module evidence.
JaguarMicro Yunxiao DPU already covers a bounded aggregate Ethernet bandwidth field while keeping protocol, virtio, cloud, security, benchmark, and operations text as boundary evidence.

NVIDIA BlueField-4 already covers the 800 Gb/s network-bandwidth catalog field while keeping STX, CMX, storage, security, benchmark, operations, SLO, memory, host-interface, and compute text as boundary evidence.

Before parent-22, Dayu Paratus 1.0 and 2.0 were listed only as DPU product pages with no captured parameters. Parent-22 updates qualitative raw comparison text but still adds no structured SKU, observation, or resolved field.

Before parent-22, Dayu Paratus was not a known source entry and did not improve the network-storage-cluster notes except as raw crawler inventory. Parent-22 records a bounded qualitative source-backed slice.

gap_reason: The local Dayu Paratus captures contain product-page text that improves the partial network-storage-cluster and hardware-accelerator boundary for DPU software/offload and cloud integration. Paratus 1.0 supports Linux on ARM SoC with DPDK/SPDK development kits, virtualization-network components, storage-client components, and OpenStack/Kubernetes integration components for bare-metal, VM, and container cloud services. Paratus 2.0 keeps the same software development/runtime environment while adding ARM SoC + FPGA architecture, FPGA network data-path processing, Dayu HPRT RDMA-adjacent protocol wording, end-to-end network data encryption wording, and upper-layer application behavior-analysis wording. The sources do not expose schema-supported numeric fields, measured performance, benchmark, production-operation, SLO, incident, or protocol-level storage-fabric proof.

promoted_boundaries.boundary: Qualitative DPU software-stack and cloud-integration evidence: Linux on ARM SoC, DPDK/SPDK development kits, host-side function offload to DPU, virtualization-network components, storage-client components, and OpenStack/Kubernetes integration components for bare-metal, VM, and container cloud services.

Qualitative DPU architecture/software boundary evidence: ARM SoC + FPGA, same software development/runtime environment as the first product, FPGA network data-path processing, HPRT RDMA-adjacent protocol wording, end-to-end network data encryption wording, and upper-layer application behavior-analysis wording.

non_promoted_boundaries: No Dayu structured compute_accelerators SKU, observation, or resolved field is added.

No Dayu network bandwidth, memory capacity, host interface, power, IOPS, latency, packet rate, throughput, benchmark, production-operation, training-throughput, inference-throughput, service-SLO, or availability claim is promoted.

Dayu DPDK, SPDK, OpenStack, Kubernetes, bare-metal cloud, VM cloud, container cloud, HPRT, FPGA network data path, encryption, and application behavior-analysis wording remains product-page boundary evidence only, not measured performance, deployed production-operation, incident-response, or protocol-level storage-fabric proof.

No Corigine, Yusur, Resnics, Asterfusion, JaguarMicro, BlueField, Atlas, Trn2, H200, Iluvatar, MetaX, Kunlunxin, Cambricon, Biren, SGLang, Kubernetes, Kueue, Volcano, data/RAG, observability, governance, cost, crawler, frontend, backend, dashboard, source-profile, or subscription row is modified as new parent-22 evidence.
